title: Clustering and Prediction of Early Capacity Fade Trajectories in Lithium-Metal Batteries using Data-Driven Approaches
authors: Maitri Uppaluri, Le Xu, Wenting Ma, Nojan Aliahmad, Karl Littau and Simona Onori
abstract: Lithium-metal batteries (LMBs) are promising candidates for next-generation batteries due to their high energy density. However, during cycling, lithium forms dendritic structures on the negative electrode surface, leading to the formation of dead lithium and resulting in capacity loss. In this paper, we employ a dataset of lab-scale lithium-metal batteries cycled under various charging rates and fixed discharge rate. We apply machine learning models that leverage initial cycle data from these cells to predict the state-of-health (SOH) trajectories. The data reveals different forms of SOH traj…

title: Long-Term Durability Projection of PEMFC Electrodes Under Non-Accelerated Conditions
authors: Anusorn Kongkanand, Paul Yu, Venkata Yarlagadda, Wenbin Gu and Srikanth Arisetty
abstract: Mathematical modeling of performance and durability plays a central role in developing electrochemical devices, where complex trade-offs between functionality, longevity, and cost are frequently done. To shorten learning cycles when developing a proton exchange membrane fuel cell (PEMFC) to last tens of thousands of hours, accelerated stress tests (ASTs) are commonly used. However, this can mischaracterize degradation mechanisms leading to unpreferred outcomes. In this work, we investigate platinum (Pt) surface area loss and carbon support degradation under non-accelerated, realistic operating…

title: Performance Study of Cellulose Acetate/Aramid Fiber Separator for Lithium Batteries
authors: Wenlong Mu, Lianqin Wang, Shikun Zhang, Wencui Guo and Kaiyuan Zhou
abstract: To address the poor thermal stability and low electrolyte affinity of commercial polyolefin separators, as well as the low mechanical strength and weak flame retardancy of pure cellulose acetate (CA) separators, two sandwich-structured composite separators, namely cellulose acetate-aramid fiber-cellulose acetate (C-A-C) and aramid fiber-cellulose acetate-aramid fiber (A-C-A), were fabricated via layer-by-layer electrospinning. The design synergistically combines the high wettability of CA with the high strength, thermal resistance, and flame retardancy of aramid fiber (AF). The composite separ…

title: pH-Dependent Redox Kinetics and Mass-Transport Parameters of CoW12 Relevant to Redox Flow Batteries
authors: Jayant Dwivedi, Geetanksha Gupta, Himanshu Kumawat, Manoj Wary and Manoj Neergat
abstract: The redox reactions of K6[CoW12O40] (CoW12)—a polyoxometalate featuring two distinct redox centers (CoO4 (Co2+/Co3+) and W12O36 (W6+/W5+))—are investigated. Cyclic voltammetry (CV) and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) are employed to study the effect of proton concentration in the pH range of 1–4 on the redox reaction kinetics and mass transport of the molecular ions in a three-electrode configuration. The charge-discharge capacity, power density, and cycling stability (reversibility) are found to be a strong function of pH. It is concluded that the pH is a critical parameter that …

title: Unravelling and Managing Thermal Behaviours of Lithium-Ion Batteries for eVTOLs via Optical Fiber Sensing
authors: Xuanhe Liang, Wu Meng, Xibin Lu and Jiaqiang Huang
abstract: Electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft (eVTOL) are poised to become a cornerstone of the low-altitude economy. However, the thermal behaviours of eVTOL batteries under mission-relevant operation remain poorly understood. Here, we implant optical-fiber sensors into commercial eVTOL cells to acquire operando internal temperature dynamics under an eVTOL duty protocol. By coupling these measurements with optical-fiber-based calorimetry, we reconstruct time-resolved heat-generation profiles with high accuracy and temporal resolution. Using the experimentally derived heat source terms, we f…

title: Resonant Flyback Converter-Based Cell-to-Cell SoC Equalization for Li-Ion Batteries in xEV Applications
authors: Sridivya Vattem, Srinivasarao Gorantla and Bharath Kumar N.
abstract: This paper describes a way to actively balance the cells in lithium-ion battery packs that are connected in series using a resonant flyback converter (RFBC). The proposed topology uses resonant energy transfer to achieve soft-switching operation and lower switching stress while keeping a single-converter architecture. Flyback-based balancing systems presented in the literature mostly use hard-switched operation or multiple converter stages. The energy flow from cells with a higher State of Charge (SoC) to a lower SoC cell is controlled by an event-driven SoC-based control algorithm which contr…

title: Urea-Derived Nitrogen as a Nitrogen Source for ZnMn2O4 Modification: Enhancing Zn2+ Transport Kinetics to Construct High-Performance Aqueous Zinc-Ion Batteries
authors: Cheng Xu, Zifan Wang, Gang Wang, Yong Lu, Qi Xu and Jingjing Qi
abstract: ZnMn2O4 (ZMO) spinel is regarded as a promising cathode material for aqueous zinc-ion batteries (AZIBs), owing to its outstanding Zn2+ storage capability. Its practical application in high-capacity cathodes is hindered by drawbacks, including low electrical conductivity, slow Zn2+ diffusion kinetics, and poor cycling stability. To address shortcomings, this study employs a nitrogen doping strategy (using three nitrogen sources) to partially substitute oxygen atoms in ZMO lattice with nitrogen atoms. Experimental and computational results demonstrate that nitrogen atoms replace oxygen atoms, fo…

title: Rigid-Flexible Coupling Strategy: 3D Polyimide Hosted Composite Electrolytes with LiDFOB for High Performance Lithium Metal Batteries
authors: Shixin Xiao, Meihua Liu and Guoqiang Li
abstract: Solid-state lithium metal batteries face critical challenges regarding ionic conductivity, mechanical strength, and interfacial instability. To address these issues, this study proposes a feasible solution: a composite solid electrolyte designed with a “rigid-flexible” coupling strategy. The electrolyte comprises a high-strength 3D porous polyimide nanofiber skeleton and a PVDF-HFP matrix filled with LATP ceramic fillers and modified by LiDFOB additives. As a result, the optimized composite exhibits remarkable performance, featuring a high tensile strength of 6.7 MPa and an excellent electroch…

title: Cathode Process in KCl-K2TiF6-TiO2 Molten Salt
abstract: In this article, the cathode process in the KCl-K2TiF6-TiO2 melt at a temperature of 800 °C was studied using cyclic voltammetry. It was shown that the titanium electroreduction on a tungsten includes three electrode stages, as evidenced by the formation of peaks on the voltammograms at potentials of −0.25, −0.90, and −1.60 V relative to the tungsten electrode potential. Presumably, these stages correspond to the transitions Ti4+ → Ti3+, Ti3+ → Ti2+ and Ti2+ → Ti°, respectively. Based on the analysis of diagnostic dependencies, it is noted that all three electrode stages are limited by the dif…

title: Tuning interfacial polarity for stable high-potential lithium metal batteries
abstract: Published online: 13 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41565-026-02167-4 A molecular engineering strategy using dipolar self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) establishes interfacial polarity as a descriptor for battery electrode stability. By tuning the electronic structure of the SAM and, thus, nanometric surface environment of the positive electrode, stable operation of high-potential lithium metal batteries is demonstrated and validated through interface-sensitive in situ spectroscopy.
